checkpoints.description: Ensure an element's role supports its ARIA attributes
checkpoints.fix: Use only ARIA attributes that are allowed for the element’s role.
checkpoints.help: Elements must only use supported ARIA attributes

nodes.html: Legal NoticePrivacy Policy

checkpoints.why: Ensures assistive technologies can correctly identify and interact with elements.

checkpoints.description: Ensure ARIA attributes are used as described in the specification of the element's role
checkpoints.fix: Review the rule documentation for repair steps.
checkpoints.help: ARIA attributes must be used as specified for the element's role

nodes.html: Legal NoticePrivacy Policy

checkpoints.why: This rule ensures compliance with general accessibility best practices.

checkpoints.description: Ensure ARIA attributes are not prohibited for an element's role
checkpoints.fix: Remove ARIA attributes that are not supported on the specific element or role.
checkpoints.help: Elements must only use permitted ARIA attributes

nodes.html: Legal NoticePrivacy Policy

checkpoints.why: Avoids confusing assistive technology with invalid or conflicting ARIA metadata.

checkpoints.description: Ensure all ARIA attributes have valid values
checkpoints.fix: Use only allowed values (e.g., true/false) for this ARIA attribute.
checkpoints.help: ARIA attributes must conform to valid values

nodes.html: Legal NoticePrivacy Policy

checkpoints.why: Incorrect values can cause screen readers to skip or misread the element state.

checkpoints.description: Ensure attributes that begin with aria- are valid ARIA attributes
checkpoints.fix: Correct the spelling or usage of the ARIA attribute.
checkpoints.help: ARIA attributes must conform to valid names

nodes.html: Legal NoticePrivacy Policy

checkpoints.why: Invalid ARIA attributes are ignored by screen readers, potentially breaking functionality.

checkpoints.description: Ensure the contentinfo landmark is at top level
checkpoints.fix: Review the rule documentation for repair steps.
checkpoints.help: Contentinfo landmark should not be contained in another landmark
checkpoints.why: This rule ensures compliance with general accessibility best practices.

checkpoints.description: Ensure the document has at most one contentinfo landmark
checkpoints.fix: Review the rule documentation for repair steps.
checkpoints.help: Document should not have more than one contentinfo landmark
checkpoints.why: This rule ensures compliance with general accessibility best practices.

checkpoints.description: Ensure landmarks are unique
checkpoints.fix: Add unique aria-labels to distinguish multiple landmarks of the same type (e.g., <nav>).
checkpoints.help: Landmarks should have a unique role or role/label/title (i.e. accessible name) combination

nodes.html: Legal NoticePrivacy Policy

checkpoints.why: Prevents confusion when multiple sections have the same role (e.g., "Main Nav" vs "Legal Nav").

checkpoints.description: Ensure links have discernible text
checkpoints.fix: Add descriptive text or an aria-label to the link.
checkpoints.help: Links must have discernible text
checkpoints.why: Users should understand where a link leads before navigating away.

checkpoints.description: Ensure touch targets have sufficient size and space
checkpoints.fix: Review the rule documentation for repair steps.
checkpoints.help: All touch targets must be 24px large, or leave sufficient space
checkpoints.why: This rule ensures compliance with general accessibility best practices.

checkpoints.description: Ensure ARIA attributes are not prohibited for an element's role
checkpoints.fix: Remove ARIA attributes that are not supported on the specific element or role.
checkpoints.help: Elements must only use permitted ARIA attributes

nodes.summary: aria-label attribute is not well supported on a span with no valid role attribute.
aria-label attribute is not well supported on a span with no valid role attribute.

checkpoints.why: Avoids confusing assistive technology with invalid or conflicting ARIA metadata.

checkpoints.description: Ensure the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds
checkpoints.fix: Increase the contrast ratio to at least 4.5:1 for normal text.
checkpoints.help: Elements must meet minimum color contrast ratio thresholds
checkpoints.why: Ensures readability for users with low vision or when using devices in bright light.

checkpoints.description: Ensure aria-hidden elements are not focusable nor contain focusable elements
checkpoints.fix: Ensure focusable elements are not hidden from assistive technologies.
checkpoints.help: ARIA hidden element must not be focusable or contain focusable elements
checkpoints.why: Prevents "ghost" focusable elements that screen reader users cannot see.

checkpoints.description: Ensure all elements with a role attribute use a valid value
checkpoints.fix: Use valid ARIA roles that match the element’s purpose.
checkpoints.help: ARIA roles used must conform to valid values
checkpoints.why: Correct roles help users understand what an element does (e.g., button vs. link).

checkpoints.description: Ensure buttons have discernible text
checkpoints.fix: Add an aria-label or visible text to the button.
checkpoints.help: Buttons must have discernible text
checkpoints.why: Users need to know what a button does before they click it.

checkpoints.description: Ensure every id attribute value used in ARIA and in labels is unique
checkpoints.fix: Remove duplicate IDs used in ARIA attributes like aria-labelledby.
checkpoints.help: IDs used in ARIA and labels must be unique
checkpoints.why: Ensures ARIA relationships remain predictable and unique.

checkpoints.description: Ensure the order of headings is semantically correct
checkpoints.fix: Ensure heading levels (h1-h6) follow a logical hierarchy.
checkpoints.help: Heading levels should only increase by one
checkpoints.why: Helps screen reader users navigate the page structure like a table of contents.

checkpoints.description: Ensure every HTML document has a lang attribute
checkpoints.fix: Add a lang attribute (e.g., lang="en") to the <html> tag.
checkpoints.help: <html> element must have a lang attribute
checkpoints.why: Enables screen readers to use the correct pronunciation and accent.

checkpoints.description: Ensure elements have alternative text or a role of none or presentation
checkpoints.fix: Add a descriptive alt attribute to the image.
checkpoints.help: Images must have alternative text
checkpoints.why: Provides a verbal description of images for blind users.

checkpoints.description: Ensure every form element has a label
checkpoints.fix: Use a <label> element or aria-label for every form input.
checkpoints.help: Form elements must have labels
checkpoints.why: Allows users to know what information is expected in a form field.

checkpoints.description: Ensure the document has a main landmark
checkpoints.fix: Ensure the page has exactly one <main> landmark.
checkpoints.help: Document should have one main landmark
checkpoints.why: Helps users skip navigation and jump directly to the primary content.

checkpoints.description: Ensure that lists are structured correctly
checkpoints.fix: Ensure <ul> or <ol> elements contain only <li> children.
checkpoints.help: <ul> and <ol> must only directly contain <li>, <script> or <template> elements
checkpoints.why: Correct list structure allows screen readers to announce the number of items.

checkpoints.description: Ensure <li> elements are used semantically
checkpoints.fix: Ensure <li> elements are contained within a <ul> or <ol>.
checkpoints.help: <li> elements must be contained in a <ul> or <ol>
checkpoints.why: Standalone list items are not recognized as part of a list by assistive tech.

checkpoints.description: Ensure all page content is contained by landmarks
checkpoints.fix: Add an aria-label to any element with a region role.
checkpoints.help: All page content should be contained by landmarks
checkpoints.why: Identifies the purpose of layout regions in the document tree.
